summary: Round 1 informational finding INF-T2-001 (forge-std no branch pin in .gitmodules) has been addressed by adding `branch = v1.16.1` to the forge-std submodule entry. All other infrastructure acceptance criteria remain satisfied. No new findings.

findings_from_round1.resolution: branch = v1.16.1 added to .gitmodules [submodule contracts/lib/forge-std] entry. Parity with OZ entry which already had branch = v5.0.2. The primary cryptographic pin remains the submodule SHA (620536fa) + foundry.lock SHA — the branch line is informational/documentation only as noted.

round2_changes_reviewed.change: Added `branch = v1.16.1` to forge-std submodule entry
round2_changes_reviewed.detail: Directly addresses INF-T2-001. Branch line is documentation-only; the commit SHA in the git index and foundry.lock remain the authoritative pins. No issues.

round2_changes_reviewed.change: DEPLOYER_KEY placeholder changed from all-zeros hex to non-key-shaped sentinel string `0x<your-0x-prefixed-64-hex-char-private-key>`
round2_changes_reviewed.detail: The comment explaining the all-zeros key risk is clear and actionable. The sentinel is non-parseable as a real private key, which eliminates the copy-paste-into-prod risk. Correct fix for a security finding from the security-auditor.

round2_changes_reviewed.change: Leading comment extended to document foundry.lock as a secondary lock alongside submodule SHA pinning
round2_changes_reviewed.detail: The added comment accurately describes forge 1.6+ foundry.lock semantics. Correctly frames it as complementary to (not replacing) the submodule SHA. Good contributor documentation.
